title: Intervals and sweep ordering

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Intervals and sweep ordering; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Intervals and sweep ordering
sources.claim: Interval algorithms first define endpoint semantics, then sort or sweep to merge, schedule, count overlap, or answer coverage.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Interval algorithms first define endpoint semantics, then sort or sweep to merge, schedule, count overlap, or answer coverage.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Half-open intervals [1,3) and [3,5) do not overlap, while closed intervals would touch at three. Your goal is to implement and test interval merging or scheduling with touching-boundary cases. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Arrays, strings and matrices; Hash tables, sets and maps; Stacks, queues and deques; Sorting properties and selection; Greedy choice and counterexamples. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Interval algorithms first define endpoint semantics, then sort or sweep to merge, schedule, count overlap, or answer coverage. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Closed versus half-open boundaries, touching intervals, and tie ordering change results. The draft therefore rejects: Intervals that share an endpoint always overlap.

includes: Sort interval events to merge, select or count overlaps under explicit boundary semantics.
